name: Yesa Reservoir Loop
name_original: Escó y el Embalse de Yesa — circular desde el Castillo de Ruesta
description: This difficult 31.8-mile touring cycling route explores the historical landscape and varied terrain around the Yesa Reservoir.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Urriés traverse a landscape characterized by its medieval heritage and diverse natural features. The region includes dramatic gorges like Foz de Arbayún and Foz de Lumbier, alongside the hilly terrain of the Sierra de Urriés. Routes often feature views of the Embalse de Yesa, offering varied elevation changes suitable for touring cyclists. This area provides a mix of cultural landmarks and natural beauty. Best touring cycling routes around Urriés The most popular touring cycling route is Yesa Reservoir Loop, a 31.8 miles (51.2 km) trail that takes 4 hours 10 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain around the reservoir. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Yesa Reservoir – Yesa Dam loop from Sigüés, a moderate 22.8 miles (36.7 km) path. This route offers views of the Yesa Reservoir and Dam with a moderate elevation profile. Local touring cyclists also love the Sangüesa Old Town – Castle of Xavier loop from Javier, a 31.6 miles (50.9 km) trail leading through historical areas and varied terrain, often completed in about 4 hours 17 minutes. Touring cycling around Urriés is defined by medieval villages, reservoir views, and the hilly terrain of the Sierra de Urriés. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to more challenging routes. The routes in Urriés are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 3 reviews. More than 20 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Urriés's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What is the general difficulty level of touring cycling routes around Urriés?
A: The touring cycling routes around Urriés are predominantly challenging, with 5 out of 6 routes rated as difficult. These routes often feature significant elevation gains, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993050991/yesa-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Yesa Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; with over 870 meters of ascent, reflecting the hilly terrain of the Sierra de Urriés. There is also one moderate route available for those seeking a less strenuous ride.

Q: Are there any moderate touring cycling routes in Urriés?
A: Yes, there is one moderate touring cycling route available: the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38163516" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Yesa Reservoir – Yesa Dam loop from Sigüés&lt;/a&gt;. This 36.7 km path offers scenic views of the Yesa Reservoir and Dam with a more manageable elevation profile, making it suitable for cyclists looking for a less demanding experience.

Q: What historical landmarks can I visit while cycling near Urriés?
A: The region around Urriés is rich in historical landmarks. Many routes pass by or offer access to significant sites. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38171863" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sangüesa Old Town – Castle of Xavier loop from Javier&lt;/a&gt; takes you through historical areas, including the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/934414" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Xavier&lt;/a&gt;. Other notable historical sites in the vicinity include the medieval historic center of Urriés itself, the Church of San Esteban, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5266483" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruesta Castle&lt;/a&gt;, which can be explored on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31848309" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruesta Castle – Ruesta Castle loop from Embalse de Yesa&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there scenic viewpoints or natural features along the cycling routes?
A: Absolutely. The touring cycling routes around Urriés are known for their picturesque natural environment. You'll encounter dramatic landscapes such as the Foz de Arbayún and Foz de Lumbier gorges, and the routes often provide beautiful views of the Embalse de Yesa (Yesa Reservoir). The hilly terrain of the Sierra de Urriés also offers numerous scenic vistas throughout your ride.

Q: Are there circular touring cycling routes available in Urriés?
A: Yes, all the touring cycling routes listed for Urriés are circular loops. This includes routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993050991/yesa-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Yesa Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20151522" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Javier Castle – Castle of Xavier loop from Sos del Rey Católico&lt;/a&gt;, allowing you to start and finish at the same point without needing to arrange return transport.

Q: When is the best time of year for touring cycling in Urriés?
A: Given the region's climate, the best time for touring cycling in Urriés is typically during the spring (April to June) and autumn (September to October) months. During these periods, the weather is generally milder and more pleasant, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the colder, potentially wetter conditions of winter. This allows for comfortable exploration of the diverse landscapes and historical sites.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy the most about touring cycling in Urriés?
A: The touring cycling routes around Urriés are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 3 reviews. Cyclists often praise the unique combination of medieval villages, stunning reservoir views, and the engaging, hilly terrain of the Sierra de Urriés, which offers a rewarding challenge.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by castles or historic settlements?
A: Several routes offer opportunities to see castles and historic settlements. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20151522" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Javier Castle – Castle of Xavier loop from Sos del Rey Católico&lt;/a&gt; specifically highlights the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5322107" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Javier Castle&lt;/a&gt; and the historic settlement of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3556493" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sos del Rey Católico&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31848309" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruesta Castle – Ruesta Castle loop from Embalse de Yesa&lt;/a&gt; also features the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5266483" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruesta Castle&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Urriés?
A: The terrain around Urriés is varied, offering a mix of experiences for touring cyclists. You can expect routes that traverse the hilly and mountainous landscapes of the Sierra de Urriés, providing challenging ascents and descents. Additionally, some paths offer views along the Embalse de Yesa, and you'll encounter areas with rich natural heritage, including forests and gorges, as well as sections through medieval villages and historic areas.

Q: Are there any routes suitable for families or beginners?
A: While most routes in Urriés are rated as difficult, there is one moderate option that might be more suitable for families with older children or beginners with some cycling experience: the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38163516" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Yesa Reservoir – Yesa Dam loop from Sigüés&lt;/a&gt;. This route offers a manageable distance and elevation, providing a good introduction to touring cycling in the area without the intensity of the more challenging trails.

Q: What are some of the natural highlights I might see while cycling?
A: Beyond the stunning views of the Embalse de Yesa, the region boasts a privileged natural heritage. Cyclists can experience the dramatic scenery of the Foz de Arbayún and Foz de Lumbier gorges, and explore the diverse ecosystems of the Sierra de Urriés, known for its rich mycological diversity. These natural features add significant beauty to the cycling experience.

Q: Are there any specific cultural or artistic points of interest along the routes?
A: Yes, the area is rich in cultural and artistic heritage. Urriés itself has a medieval historic center with the Romanesque Church of San Esteban. Nearby, you can find the Monastery of Leyre and an Interpretation Center for Romanesque Paintings, which highlights frescoes from Urriés, Ruesta, and Bagüés, with those from Bagüés often referred to as the 'Sistine Chapel of the Romanesque'. These sites offer unique cultural stops during your cycling tour.
tips: We come across a beautiful picture offered by the medieval castle of Ruesta on top of a stony hill, from where there are good views of the Yesa reservoir. It dates from the 11th century and was an important defensive enclave of the kingdom of Aragon during the Reconquest period. Currently, only two towers remain.

Escó or Esco is located to the north of the province of Zaragoza and it was in 1960 that most of the area was expropriated (together with those of Ruesta and Tiermas) for the construction of the Yesa reservoir, with which the town began its decline to abandonment. Now it gives us impressive views of the reservoir between the old and dilapidated houses.
Big dam built over the older and smaller dam. This is the reason why you should ride as soon as you can here as when the lake will be raised everything will disappear

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Esco and the Yesa Reservoir circular cycling route?
faqs.answer: This touring bicycle route is quite extensive, covering over 51 kilometers. While individual times vary, komoot estimates the duration to be around 4 hours and 10 minutes of active cycling. However, with stops to explore the historical sites and enjoy the views, you should plan for a longer day.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the cycling route around Esco and the Yesa Reservoir?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied and can be challenging. Expect a mix of surfaces; while some stretches may be well-paved, there are likely unpaved segments, especially in areas with clay-rich ground. These unpaved sections can become very muddy and difficult to ride in wet weather. The route also features steep sections, particularly when descending through the remnants of Ruesta village, which may require pushing your bike at times.

faqs.question: What historical sites and natural landmarks can I see along the way?
faqs.answer: This route is rich in both history and natural beauty. You'll start near Ruesta Castle, an 11th-century fortress offering fantastic views. As you cycle, you'll encounter the evocative Ruins of Escó, an abandoned village with decaying structures set against the reservoir. Further along, you'll pass the impressive Yesa Dam, which created the expansive Yesa Reservoir, often called the "sea of the Pyrenees."

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners or families with children?
faqs.answer: Given its 'difficult' grading, significant distance (over 51 km), and considerable elevation gain (873 meters), this route is not recommended for beginners or young families. The varied terrain, including potentially muddy and steep sections, requires a good level of fitness and cycling experience.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start the cycling route from Ruesta Castle?
faqs.answer: The route begins from Ruesta Castle. While specific parking facilities aren't detailed, it's generally possible to find parking in or near the village of Ruesta, close to the castle remnants, which serves as the primary trailhead for this circular route.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: To avoid challenging conditions, it's best to cycle this route during drier seasons. Spring and autumn typically offer pleasant temperatures and less rainfall, reducing the likelihood of encountering very muddy, clay-rich sections. Summers can be hot, while winters might bring colder temperatures and increased chances of wet weather.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While there are no specific restrictions mentioned, generally, dogs are welcome on outdoor cycling routes in this region. However, due to the length, varied terrain, and potential for encountering wildlife or other cyclists, it's advisable to keep your dog on a leash and ensure they are fit enough for the distance.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to cycle this trail?
faqs.answer: No specific permits or entrance fees are required to access or cycle this public route around the Yesa Reservoir and through the historical sites like Escó and Ruesta. However, always respect private property and local regulations.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with any other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, parts of this route coincide with other established paths. You will find sections that are also part of the Aragonese Way and the Via Sancti Martini - Chemin de Saragosse. The Camino de Santiago also passes along the banks of the Yesa Reservoir in the general area.

faqs.question: Are there any viewpoints or particularly scenic spots along the route?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. The route offers remarkable scenery throughout. From Ruesta Castle, you'll get fantastic panoramic views of the Yesa Reservoir. Cycling past the Ruins of Escó provides a striking and somewhat melancholic landscape, with the decaying structures set against the vast reservoir. The entire area around the Yesa Reservoir, at the foot of the Leire Range, is highly recommended for its natural beauty.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a touring bicycle trip on this route?
faqs.answer: Given the route's length and difficulty, it's crucial to be well-prepared. Bring plenty of water and snacks, as services may be limited. A repair kit, spare inner tubes, and a pump are essential. Due to varied terrain, including potentially muddy sections, consider a touring bicycle with robust tires. Appropriate clothing for changing weather conditions, sun protection, and a first-aid kit are also highly recommended.
